name: Leonard Bernstein
abstract: Leonard Bernstein ( BURN-styne; born Louis Bernstein; August 25, 1918 – October 14, 1990) was an American conductor, composer, pianist, music educator, author, and humanitarian. Considered to be one of the most important conductors of his time, he was the first American-born conductor to receive international acclaim.

about_quotes.speaker: Marian McPartland
about_quotes.evidence: I never did meet him. I loved all that music. I went to see the West Side Story.
about_quotes.doc_title: Marian McPartland — Smithsonian Jazz Oral History

about_quotes.speaker: John Beal
about_quotes.evidence: And I played a couple of things with him. I played on a mass that he wrote, a recording, I played actually Fender bass on that. And there was a ballet piece he wrote called “Dybbuk” that I played on. And we did “Symphony of Psalms” at Carnegie Hall one time that he conducted.
about_quotes.doc_title: John Beal interviewed by Monk Rowe, via Zoom, June 18, 2024

about_quotes.speaker: Jack Lesberg
about_quotes.evidence: I met Leonard Bernstein there... he says why don't you go and audition for the — he was taking over the City Center Symphony... He actually put me on as Associate Principal when I went with the orchestra, with that City Central Symphony when I went with him.
about_quotes.doc_title: Jack Lesberg interviewed by Monk Rowe, Aspen, Colorado, October 11, 1997

about_quotes.speaker: Dan Morgenstern
about_quotes.evidence: The titular was Leonard Bernstein, who came in once a week. He was known as the airborne professor, because he flew in from New York to give a lecture which was attended by practically everybody, because he was a brilliant lecturer.
about_quotes.doc_title: Dan Morgenstern — Smithsonian Jazz Oral History

about_quotes.speaker: Manny Albam
about_quotes.evidence: I got a call and a guy said, “this is Leonard Bernstein” he said, “I want to really congratulate you on what you did.” ... Bernstein asked me, he said, “do you ever write anything, would you ever write anything for my big band”
about_quotes.doc_title: Manny Albam interviewed by Monk Rowe, Tarrytown, New York, October 31, 1998

about_quotes.speaker: Harry Edison
about_quotes.evidence: That’s where you go to hear Marian Anderson, and hear La Traviata, and Leonard Bernstein.
about_quotes.doc_title: Harry Edison — Smithsonian Jazz Oral History
